meta.title: Post-operative IQ > 70 after paediatric epilepsy surgery (Cloppenborg)
meta.authors: Cloppenborg T, van Schooneveld MMJ, Hagemann A, Hopf JL, Kalbhenn T, Otte WM, Polster T, Bien CG, Braun KPJ
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. The probability that a child will have an intelligence quotient above 70 two years after paediatric epilepsy surgery, from pre-surgical IQ and the extent of the resection. Unlike the IQ > 85 model, invasive diagnostics are not part of this model.

Validation. Developed from 492 children with IQ/DQ measured before and 2 years after surgery (IQ n=365, DQ n=127). The IQ models were developed in the Bielefeld (Bethel) cohort (n=258) and externally validated in the Utrecht cohort (n=102): accuracy 0.82 (IQ>85) and 0.84 (IQ>70), with excellent discrimination (AUC 0.90 and 0.92). The DQ model was developed in the Bethel cohort with 10-fold cross-validation (correct classification 76%). Notably, the risk of cognitive deterioration after surgery was very low (IQ: 37.3% improved vs 6.6% deteriorated).

Source & acknowledgement. This calculator reproduces the Epilepsy Prediction Tools model developed jointly by Universitätsklinik OWL (Bielefeld) and UMC Utrecht. The published look-up tables were taken from the authors' own reference implementation (github.com/wmotte/epilepsypredictiontools, Apache-2.0) and re-expressed exactly as the underlying additive logistic model. Please cite the original paper and visit epilepsypredictiontools.info for the authors' original tools.

predictors.name: Pre-surgical IQ
predictors.assess: Intelligence quotient measured before surgery (41–140). The strongest predictor of post-operative IQ; modelled as a spline because the effect is non-linear.

predictors.name: Type of epilepsy surgery
predictors.assess: Extent of the resection. Multilobar resection or hemispherectomy lowers the predicted chance of a good intellectual outcome compared with a focal resection.

model.panel_title: Intellectual outcome 2 years after paediatric epilepsy surgery
model.note: Population-level estimate from a 2-year post-surgical outcome model. Always apply clinical judgement.
model.viz_info: The pie shows the predicted probability of a post-operative IQ > 70. Below, the waterfall breaks down how each factor moves that chance up or down from the baseline.

provenance.source: Cloppenborg et al., Neurology 2022;98(3):e225-e235 (doi 10.1212/WNL.0000000000013065, PMID 34795046). Published look-up table taken verbatim from the authors' reference implementation at github.com/wmotte/epilepsypredictiontools (Apache-2.0), as published at epilepsypredictiontools.info. The table is a materialisation of an additive logistic model: inverting the logit recovers the model exactly (category effects are constant across the whole axis to <3e-4 on the log-odds scale, i.e. rounding of the published 9-decimal probabilities). Reproduced here as a spline on the pre-operative score plus constant category effects.
provenance.notes: Additive logistic model recovered by inverting the authors' published probability table. Spline = logit p at the reference profile (focal resection) across IQ 41-140; multilobar/hemispherectomy enters as a constant log-odds offset.
